title: Expressing actions in progress with 'zai'
explanation: Expressing actions in progress with 'zai'. Used for describing actions, indicating conditions, referring to the present.

examples.english: Who is taking a bath inside?

examples.chinese: 阿姨正在打扫我们的房间。
examples.pinyin: āyí zhèngzài dǎsǎo wǒmen de fángjiān 。
examples.english: The aunt is cleaning our room.

examples.chinese: 昨天晚上七点，我们在吃饭。
examples.english: At 7 o'clock last night, we were eating.

examples.chinese: 我现在在上班，不方便离开。
examples.english: I'm at work now and it's inconvenient to leave.

title: Negation of 'you' with 'mei'
explanation: Negation of 'you' with 'mei'. Used for negating, denying and refusing.

examples.english: We don't have a house in Beijing.

title: Standard negation with 'bu'
explanation: Standard negation with 'bu'. Used for negating, describing actions, denying and refusing, referring to habitual actions, referring to the present, referring to the future.

examples.english: I don't want to work today.

examples.chinese: 为什么你不喜欢喝咖啡？
examples.pinyin: wèishíyāo nǐ bù xǐhuān hē kāfēi ？

title: The 'all' adverb 'dou'
explanation: The 'all' adverb 'dou'. Used for emphasizing, expressing quantity.

title: The 'also' adverb 'ye'
explanation: The 'also' adverb 'ye'. Used for adding more explanation.

examples.pinyin: tāmen yě shì fǎguó rén ma ？

examples.english: I also want to learn Chinese.

title: Asking about degree with 'duo'
explanation: Asking about degree with 'duo'. Used for asking questions, adding more explanation, expressing quality.

examples.english: How long is the Yellow River?

examples.pinyin: nǐ jiā lí zhèér duō yuǎn ？
examples.english: How far is your home from here?

title: Expressing 'not very' with 'bu tai'
explanation: Expressing 'not very' with 'bu tai'. Used for adding more explanation.

examples.english: My home is not too big.

examples.pinyin: nà gè dìfāng bù tài yuǎn 。
examples.english: That place is not too far away.
The boss is not very happy today.

examples.pinyin: zhè gè diàn de yīfú bù tài gùi 。
examples.english: The clothes in this shop are not too expensive.

examples.pinyin: wǒ juédé tā bù tài cōngmíng 。
examples.english: I think he is not very smart.

title: Expressing 'and' with 'he'
explanation: Expressing 'and' with 'he'. Used for listing.

examples.english: The boss likes coffee and tea.

examples.pinyin: wǒ de yéyé hé nǎinǎi dū 70 sùi 。
examples.english: My grandpa and grandma are both 70 years old.

examples.chinese: 他和他女朋友都喜欢中国菜。
examples.pinyin: tā hé tā nv̌péngyǒu dū xǐhuān zhōngguó cài 。
examples.english: He and his girlfriend love Chinese food.

examples.chinese: 你爸爸和你妈妈都是美国人吗？
examples.pinyin: nǐ bàbà hé nǐ māmā dū shì měiguó rén ma ？
examples.english: Are your father and mother both American?

title: After a specific time with 'yihou'
explanation: After a specific time with 'yihou'. Used for sequencing events in time, expressing time and date.

examples.chinese: 下午三点以后，我不在家。
examples.pinyin: xiàwǔ sāndiǎn yǐhòu， wǒ bù zài jiā 。
examples.english: After three in the afternoon, I am not at home.

examples.chinese: 来中国以后，她认识了她的老公。
examples.pinyin: lái zhōngguó yǐhòu， tā rènshì le tā de lǎogōng 。
examples.english: After coming to China, she met her husband.

examples.chinese: 她一个月以后开始上班。
examples.english: She started work one month later.

examples.chinese: 吃完午饭以后，我们要开会。
examples.pinyin: chī wán wǔfàn yǐhòu ， wǒmen yào kāihùi 。
examples.english: After lunch, we have a meeting.

examples.chinese: 几年以后，我们公司会更大。
examples.pinyin: jīnián yǐhòu ，wǒmen gōngsī hùi gèng dà 。
examples.english: In a few years, our company will be bigger.

title: Before a specific time with 'yiqian'
explanation: Before a specific time with 'yiqian'. Used for referring to the past, sequencing events in time, expressing time and date.

examples.pinyin: chīfàn yǐqián ，nǐ xǐ shǒu le ma ？
examples.english: Did you wash your hands before eating?
Don't eat anything before going to bed.

examples.pinyin: liǎng nián yǐqián ， nǐ rènshì tā ma ？
examples.english: Did you know him two years ago?

examples.chinese: 星期五以前，你要做完这些工作。
examples.pinyin: xīngqīwǔ yǐqián ，nǐ yào zuò wán zhèxiē gōngzuò 。
examples.english: Before Friday, you have to finish these tasks.

examples.chinese: 上大学以前，你来过上海吗？
examples.pinyin: shàng dàxué yǐqián，nǐ lái guò shànghǎi ma ？
examples.english: Have you been to Shanghai before going to university?

title: Time words and word order
structure: Subj. + Time + Verb + Obj. / Time + Subj. + Verb + Obj.
explanation: Time words and word order. Used for sentence patterns, sequencing events in time, expressing time and date.

examples.english: I went to the bar yesterday.
I went to the bar yesterday.

examples.pinyin: xià gè xīngqī tā yào húi guó 。
examples.english: He will return to China next week.

examples.pinyin: tā xià gè xīngqī yào húi guó 。
examples.english: He will return home next week.

examples.pinyin: míngnián wǒ yào kāi yī gè gōngsī 。
examples.english: I will start a company next year.

title: Age with 'sui'
explanation: Age with 'sui'. Used for introducing people, describing people.

examples.english: My son is one year old.
My mother is 45 years old this year.
Is your grandpa 80 years old this year?

title: Measure word 'ge'
explanation: Measure word 'ge'. Used for expressing quantity.

examples.chinese: 十个男人，七个傻，八个坏。
examples.pinyin: shí gè nánrén， qī gè shǎ， bā gè huài 。
examples.english: Ten men, seven silly, and eight bad.

examples.pinyin: nǐ xiǎng chī gè bāozǐ ma ？
examples.english: Do you want to eat a bun?

title: Structure of dates
structure: x 年 + y 月 + z （日 /  号）
explanation: Structure of dates. Used for expressing time and date.

examples.english: We will go to Shanghai on October 1.

title: Structure of times (advanced)
structure: x 点 零 y 分
explanation: Structure of times (advanced). Used for expressing time and date.

title: Structure of times (basic)
structure: Hour + 点 + 差 + Minutes + 分
explanation: Structure of times (basic). Used for expressing time and date.

examples.pinyin: chà wǔ fēn bā diǎn bàn

title: Expressing close possession without 'de'
explanation: Expressing close possession without 'de'. Used for expressing possession.

title: Expressing completion with 'le'
explanation: Expressing completion with 'le'. Used for describing actions, referring to the past, expressing completion.

examples.chinese: 他爸爸是我们公司的老板。
examples.pinyin: tā bàbà shì wǒmen gōngsī de lǎobǎn 。
examples.english: His father is the boss of our company.

title: Expressing 'not anymore' with 'le'
structure: Subj. + Verb + 了 + [Number + Measure Word] + Obj.
explanation: Expressing 'not anymore' with 'le'. Used for referring to changes of situation.

examples.pinyin: wǒ hē le liǎng bēi kāfēi 。
examples.english: I drank two cups of coffee.

examples.pinyin: wǒ hē le liǎng bēi kāfēi 。
examples.english: I drank two cups of coffee.
I don't want to eat anymore.

examples.pinyin: nǐ bù xǐhuān wǒ le ma ？

title: Expressing 'now' with 'le'
structure: 已经 + 不 + [Verb Phrase] + 了
explanation: Expressing 'now' with 'le'. Used for referring to changes of situation.

examples.pinyin: tāmen yǐjīng bù zài yīqǐ le 。

title: Expressing possession with 'de'
explanation: Expressing possession with 'de'. Used for expressing possession.

examples.english: Hurry up, it's time for a meeting!

title: Questions with 'ne'
explanation: Questions with 'ne'. Used for asking questions, expressing attitude.

examples.pinyin: zhè gè hěn hǎo ， nà gè ní ？
examples.english: This is good, what about that?

examples.pinyin: zhè gè hěn hǎo ， nà gè ní ？
examples.english: This is good, what about that?

examples.pinyin: zhè gè yòng zhōngwén zěnyāo shuō ？
examples.english: How do you say this in Chinese?

title: Softening speech with 'ba'
explanation: Softening speech with 'ba'. Used for expressing attitude.

title: Suggestions with 'ba'
explanation: Suggestions with 'ba'. Used for requesting, expressing attitude.

examples.pinyin: tài gùi le ba ， wǒ bù mǎi le 。
examples.english: It's too expensive, I won't buy it.

examples.chinese: 那个地方太远了吧，我不想去。
examples.pinyin: nà gè dìfāng tài yuǎn le ba，wǒ bù xiǎng qù 。
examples.english: That place is too far away, I don't want to go.

title: Directional verbs 'lai' and 'qu'
explanation: Directional verbs 'lai' and 'qu'. Used for expressing movement.

examples.english: Mom is going to the supermarket.
Does the boss come to the company today?

title: Expressing existence in a place with 'zai'
explanation: Expressing existence in a place with 'zai'. Used for indicating conditions, expressing existence, expressing location.

examples.english: Dad will go on a business trip to Beijing tomorrow.

examples.chinese: 我今天不上班，你们可以来我家吃饭。
examples.pinyin: wǒ jīntiān bù shàngbān，nǐmen kěyǐ lái wǒ jiā chīfàn 。
examples.english: I don't go to work today, you can come to my house for dinner.

title: Expressing existence with 'you'
explanation: Expressing existence with 'you'. Used for indicating conditions, describing places, expressing existence.

examples.english: My friend and I are at the bar.

examples.pinyin: wǒ jiā yǒu hěn duō xiǎo gǒu 。
examples.english: There are many puppies in my family.

examples.pinyin: wǒmen xuéxiào yǒu hěn duō shuàigē 。
examples.english: There are many handsome guys in our school.
There are many Chinese in Japan.

title: Expressing possession with 'you'
explanation: Expressing possession with 'you'. Used for expressing possession.

examples.chinese: 我的手机里有你的号码。
examples.pinyin: wǒ de shǒujī lǐ yǒu nǐ de hàomǎ 。
examples.english: I have your number in my mobile phone.

examples.pinyin: xiànzài bàngōngshì lǐ yǒu rén ma ？
examples.english: Is anyone in the office now?

title: Polite requests with 'qing'
explanation: Polite requests with 'qing'. Used for requesting.

title: Using the verb 'jiao'
explanation: Using the verb 'jiao'. Used for introducing people.

examples.english: My brother is called Lao Ma.

title: Using the verb 'qu'
explanation: Using the verb 'qu'. Used for expressing movement.

examples.english: This place is called the Bund.
Are you going to the bathroom?
In the afternoon I will go to the supermarket.

title: Expressing ability or possibility with 'neng'
explanation: Expressing ability or possibility with 'neng'. Used for expressing possibility, expressing ability.

examples.english: We will buy it at nine.

examples.pinyin: wǒ néng chī sì shí gè jiǎozǐ 。
nǐ néng bāng wǒ zhǎodào tā ma ？
examples.english: Can you help me find him?

title: Expressing a learned skill with 'hui'
explanation: Expressing a learned skill with 'hui'. Used for expressing ability.

examples.chinese: 上课的时候不能说英文。
examples.pinyin: shàngkè de shíhòu bù néng shuō yīngwén 。
examples.english: English cannot be spoken during class.

examples.chinese: 工作的时候不能玩手机。
examples.pinyin: gōngzuò de shíhòu bù néng wán shǒujī 。
examples.english: Can't play mobile phone while working.

title: Expressing 'will' with 'hui'
structure: Subj. + 不 + 会 + Verb + Obj.
explanation: Expressing 'will' with 'hui'. Used for referring to the future, sequencing events in time, expressing ability.

examples.english: My grandma can't use a computer.

examples.pinyin: tā hùi lái kàn nǐ ma ？
examples.english: Will he come to see you?
is it going to rain tomorrow?

title: Expressing 'would like to' with 'xiang'
structure: Subj. + 不 + 会 + Verb + Obj.
explanation: Expressing 'would like to' with 'xiang'. Used for expressing desires, expressing purpose.

examples.pinyin: tā bù hùi gēn nǐ jiéhūn 。

examples.chinese: 今晚我不会在外面吃饭。
examples.pinyin: jīnwǎn wǒ bù hùi zài wàimiàn chīfàn 。
examples.english: I will not eat out tonight.
Do you want to drink water?

title: How to do something with 'zenme'
structure: Subj. + 不 + 想 + Verb + Obj.
explanation: How to do something with 'zenme'. Used for asking questions.

examples.chinese: 那个地方很近，我不想开车。
examples.pinyin: nàgè dìfāng hěn jìn，wǒ bù xiǎng kāichē 。
examples.english: That place is close, I don't want to drive.

examples.pinyin: tā bù xiǎng huā fùmǔ de qián 。
examples.english: He didn't want to spend his parents' money.
How do you go to work?

examples.pinyin: nǐ zhīdào zěnyāo qù wàitān ma ？
examples.english: Do you know how to get to the Bund?

title: Indicating location with 'zai' before verbs
explanation: Indicating location with 'zai' before verbs. Used for expressing location.

examples.english: I go to university in Shanghai.

examples.chinese: 你一直在这家公司工作吗？
examples.pinyin: nǐ yīzhí zài zhè jiā gōngsī gōngzuò ma ？
examples.english: Have you always worked in this company?

examples.pinyin: wǒ zhōumò xiǎng zài jiā shùijué 。
examples.english: I want to sleep at home on the weekend.

title: Negation of past actions with 'meiyou'
explanation: Negation of past actions with 'meiyou'. Used for denying and refusing, referring to the past.

examples.english: The boss meets the customer in the meeting room.

examples.chinese: 你在外面吃过晚饭了吗？
examples.pinyin: nǐ zài wàimiàn chī guò wǎnfàn le ma ？
wǒ méiyǒu hē nǐ de píjǐu 。

title: Connecting nouns with 'shi'
explanation: Connecting nouns with 'shi'. Used for introducing people, describing people, expressing existence, referring to the present.

title: Expressing 'excessively' with 'tai'
explanation: Expressing 'excessively' with 'tai'. Used for emphasizing, expressing quality.

examples.pinyin: nǐ yǒu liǎng gè háizǐ，shì ma ？
examples.english: You have two children, are you?

title: Simple 'noun + adjective' sentences
explanation: Simple 'noun + adjective' sentences. Used for describing things, expressing quality.

examples.pinyin: nǐ de háizǐ tài cōngmíng le 。

title: Expressing 'some' with 'yixie'
explanation: Expressing 'some' with 'yixie'. Used for expressing quantity.

examples.chinese: 妈妈去超市买了一些水果。
examples.pinyin: māmā qù chāoshì mǎi le yīxiē shǔiguǒ 。
examples.english: Mother went to the supermarket and bought some fruits.

examples.chinese: 上个周末他买了一些衣服。
examples.pinyin: shàng gè zhōumò tā mǎile yīxiē yīfú 。
examples.english: He bought some clothes last weekend.

examples.chinese: 我们很快会见到一些新同事。
examples.pinyin: wǒmen hěn kuài hùi jiàndào yīxiē xīn tóngshì 。
examples.english: We will soon meet some new colleagues.

title: Counting money
explanation: Counting money. Used for expressing quantity.

examples.chinese: 你想在咖啡里放一些糖吗？
examples.pinyin: nǐ xiǎng zài kāfēi lǐ fàng yīxiē táng ma ？
examples.english: Do you want to put some sugar in the coffee?

examples.chinese: 我在中国的时候，去过一些很漂亮的地方。
examples.pinyin: wǒ zài zhōngguó de shíhòu，qù guò yīxiē hěn piāoliàng de dìfāng 。
examples.english: When I was in China, I visited some beautiful places.
How much is your mobile phone?

title: Measure words in quantity questions
explanation: Measure words in quantity questions. Used for asking questions.

examples.english: How many children does he have?

examples.pinyin: nǐ jiā yǒu jī gè fángjiān ？
examples.english: How many rooms does your family have?

examples.chinese: 他们在这里住几个星期？
examples.pinyin: tāmen zài zhèlǐ zhù jī gè xīngqī ？
examples.english: How many weeks do they live here?

title: Asking how something is with 'zenmeyang'
structure: Subj. + Verb + 多少 + Measure Word + Noun ？
explanation: Asking how something is with 'zenmeyang'. Used for asking questions, expressing attitude.

examples.chinese: 他们昨天请了多少个朋友？
examples.pinyin: tāmen zuótiān qǐng le duōshǎo gè péngyǒu ？
examples.english: How many friends did they invite yesterday?

examples.chinese: 上海有多少个外国公司？
examples.pinyin: shànghǎi yǒu duōshǎo gè wàiguó gōngsī ？
examples.english: How many foreign companies are there in Shanghai?

title: Placement of question words
structure: 你 觉得 + Topic + 怎么样 ?
explanation: Placement of question words. Used for sentence patterns, asking questions.

examples.english: What do you think about me?
What do you think of Chinese?
what dishes do you like to eat?

title: Questions with 'le ma'
explanation: Questions with 'le ma'. Used for asking questions.

examples.chinese: 你男朋友找到新工作了吗？
examples.pinyin: nǐnánpéngyǒu zhǎodào xīn gōngzuò le ma ？
examples.english: Has your boyfriend found a new job?

title: Yes-no questions with 'ma'
explanation: Yes-no questions with 'ma'. Used for asking questions, requesting.

examples.pinyin: zhè gè diànyǐng nǐ kàn le ma ？
wǒ de yóujiàn nǐ shōudào le ma ？

title: The 'shi... de' construction for emphasizing details
explanation: The 'shi... de' construction for emphasizing details. Used for referring to the past, emphasizing.

examples.pinyin: wǒ shì zuò huǒchē qù de 。

title: Comparing 'bu' and 'mei'
structure: Subj. + 是 + [Information to be Emphasized] + Verb + 的
explanation: Comparing 'bu' and 'mei'. Used for negating, describing actions, denying and refusing.

examples.pinyin: nàcì wǒmen shì yòng Skype kāi hùi de 。
examples.english: We used Skype for the meeting.

examples.pinyin: wǒmen píngshí shì yòng Skype kāi hùi de 。
examples.english: We usually use Skype for meetings.
Dad doesn't come back for dinner.
The boss will not come tomorrow.
